file_insights.details: Added InstallHooks variant to Command enum with documentation comment describing filesystem event hook installation for event-driven merge-gate
file_insights.impact: Minor - adds new CLI command surface without breaking existing functionality

patterns_discovered.description: New command variant added with placeholder handler that prints eprintln and returns Ok(). This is used as a scaffolding approach for incomplete features
patterns_discovered.alignment_to_codebase: matches existing codebase style of deferring implementation

patterns_discovered.description: Command enum uses comprehensive pattern matching with dedicated match arms for each variant. InstallHooks follows this established structure

gotchas_discovered.description: InstallHooks command is not functionally implemented - only prints error message and returns success. Will confuse users if invoked expecting actual hook installation
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: TODO comment present; implementation required before production use

gotchas_discovered.description: Command returns Ok(()) despite not being implemented. Users will see no error, only the eprintln stderr message
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Could return Err() for better CLI feedback, but current approach flags intent clearly via eprintln

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully added InstallHooks command variant to Command enum with matching handler in main function
approach_outcome.integration_status: compiles successfully, ready for implementation phase

recommendations.recommendation: Implement hook installation logic in InstallHooks match arm. Based on memory context, this should configure filesystem event hooks (WatchPaths) for event-driven merge-gate workflow
Consider returning Err() instead of Ok(()) when handler is unimplemented, to provide better CLI UX and prevent silent failures
Add examples or help text showing InstallHooks usage once implementation is complete
